message:
feat(tui): zc on leaf closes nearest enclosing fold (vim semantics)

Root cause: tree_collapse_current only acted when the cursor was on a
node with children. On a leaf (or a grandchild whose parent was already
collapsed) it silently no-oped, which diverges from vim's zc behavior
where the cursor line's enclosing fold is what closes.

Fix: when the cursor is not on an open foldable node, walk up the flat
DFS list to the nearest preceding entry with `depth < cursor.depth` and
`has_children`, collapse that ancestor, then move the cursor to the
newly closed row (looked up by issue id since the flat list is rebuilt).
This means repeat `zc` now walks up the ancestry chain one fold per
press — same as vim. An orphan leaf at the root still no-ops gracefully.

Uses the existing depth column on TreeFlatNode instead of rebuilding a
parent-child map; the DFS invariant in build_tree_flat_nodes guarantees
the nearest preceding shallower row is the parent.

Adds four unit tests covering:
  - leaf -> parent collapse + cursor move
  - grandchild -> nearest ancestor (not root) collapse
  - repeat zc walks up (C2 -> P)
  - regression guard: zc on open foldable parent still collapses in place
  - orphan root leaf no-ops without panic

Closes #11

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
